seoTitle: SouthChip Power Management Solutions | Battery Charging Systems | LiTong
seoDescription: Explore SouthChip power management solutions for consumer electronics, industrial, and automotive applications. Complete battery charging and management system designs.

solutions.title: Smartphone Power Management System
solutions.description: Complete power management solution for smartphones featuring fast charging, fuel gauging, and multi-rail DC-DC conversion using SouthChip battery management and power ICs.
solutions.longDescription: This smartphone power management solution leverages SouthChip's comprehensive portfolio of battery management and power ICs to create a complete power subsystem. The solution integrates fast battery charging, accurate fuel gauging, and multiple DC-DC converters for various system rails. The SC8815 switching charger provides up to 3A fast charging with high efficiency, while the SC2731 fuel gauge delivers accurate battery monitoring with impedance tracking technology. Multiple SC8105 buck converters generate various system voltages (3.3V, 1.8V, 1.2V) with high efficiency. The SC5328 power path management IC ensures seamless switching between battery and USB power while managing the charging process. This integrated approach reduces BOM cost, minimizes PCB area, and simplifies system design. The solution supports USB BC1.2 and USB-C charging with automatic adapter detection. As an authorized SouthChip distributor, LiTong provides complete solution support including reference designs, schematic review, and debugging assistance.

faeInsights.content: I've implemented this smartphone power management solution in multiple designs. The integration of SouthChip ICs works very well - the SC8815 charger communicates seamlessly with the SC2731 fuel gauge for optimal charging. Key design considerations: Place the charger IC close to the battery connector to minimize charging path resistance. The fuel gauge sense resistor should be placed between battery negative and system ground with Kelvin connections for accurate current measurement. For thermal management, ensure adequate copper area under the SC8815 - it can handle 3A but needs good heat dissipation. The I2C interface allows the AP to monitor and control all power functions. One tip: implement proper input current limiting in software to prevent overloading weak USB sources. Overall, this is a robust, cost-effective solution for smartphone power management.
keyTakeaways: SC8815 and SC2731 work seamlessly together
Proper PCB layout critical for thermal performance
Kelvin connections essential for accurate current sensing
Software input current limiting prevents USB overloading

steps: Define charge current and system rail requirements
Select SC8815 for charger and SC2731 for fuel gauge
Size SC8105 buck converters for each system rail
Add SC5328 for power path management
Design PCB with proper thermal management

faqs.question: What is the total solution size?
faqs.answer: The smartphone power management solution is compact: (1) PCB area - Approximately 15mm x 20mm for all power management components including charger, fuel gauge, and DC-DC converters. (2) Component count - 4 main ICs plus passive components (inductors, capacitors, resistors). Total ~25-30 components. (3) Height profile - All components under 1mm height except inductors (typically 1-1.2mm). (4) Comparison - Significantly smaller than discrete solutions requiring separate charger, gauge, and multiple converters. The high integration of SouthChip ICs minimizes solution size. WLCSP package options available for space-constrained designs. LiTong can provide reference layout and miniaturization guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for solution size optimization and compact layout design.

faqs.question: How do I implement fast charging?
faqs.answer: Fast charging implementation with SouthChip solution: (1) High current - SC8815 supports up to 3A charge current for fast charging. Set current based on battery capability and thermal limits. (2) Input power - Ensure adapter can supply required power (e.g., 9V/2A for 18W charging). SC8815 accepts up to 12V input. (3) Thermal management - Monitor battery and PCB temperature. Reduce current if temperature exceeds safe limits. (4) Charge profile - Use constant-current fast charge to ~80% SOC, then taper to constant-voltage. (5) Safety - Implement multiple safety checks including voltage, current, and temperature monitoring. (6) Communication - Use I2C to monitor charging status and adjust parameters. The SC8815 includes thermal regulation to automatically reduce current if overheating. Proper thermal design allows sustained fast charging without damage.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for fast charging implementation and thermal design guidance.

faqs.question: What is the development timeline for Smartphone Power Management System?
faqs.answer: Development timeline for Smartphone Power Management System: (1) Evaluation phase - 1-2 weeks to evaluate reference design and test key features. (2) Schematic design - 1-2 weeks to adapt reference design to your requirements. (3) PCB layout - 1-2 weeks for layout and review. (4) Prototype build - 1-2 weeks for PCB fabrication and assembly. (5) Bring-up and debug - 2-3 weeks for initial testing and issue resolution. (6) Optimization - 1-2 weeks for performance tuning. Total timeline typically 7-13 weeks from start to working prototype. LiTong can accelerate this with reference designs, schematic review, and debugging support. Contact us for project planning assistance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for project planning and accelerated development support.

faqs.question: What testing is required for Smartphone Power Management System?
faqs.answer: Testing requirements for Smartphone Power Management System: (1) Functional testing - Verify all power rails, charging, and protection features. (2) Performance testing - Measure efficiency, transient response, and ripple. (3) Thermal testing - Verify operation across temperature range and thermal limits. (4) Safety testing - Validate all protection features (OVP, OCP, OTP). (5) EMC testing - Conducted and radiated emissions testing. (6) Reliability testing - Long-term operation and environmental stress testing. (7) Compatibility testing - USB charging compatibility with various adapters and devices. LiTong can provide test procedures and support for validation testing. Reference designs include recommended test points and procedures.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for test procedures and validation support.

faqs.question: Can Smartphone Power Management System be customized?
faqs.answer: Smartphone Power Management System can be customized for specific requirements: (1) Charge current - Programmable charge current to match battery specifications. (2) System rails - Adjustable output voltages for different processor requirements. (3) Protection thresholds - Configurable protection limits for specific applications. (4) Communication interface - I2C configuration for host control and monitoring. (5) Size optimization - Component selection and layout optimization for space constraints. (6) Cost optimization - Alternative component selection for cost-sensitive applications. (7) Feature addition - Additional features like wireless charging, multiple USB ports. LiTong FAEs can help customize the solution while maintaining reliability and performance. Contact us for customization requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for solution customization and optimization.

solutions.title: Power Bank Battery Management System
solutions.description: Complete battery management solution for power banks featuring bidirectional charging, high-efficiency conversion, and accurate capacity indication using SouthChip power management ICs.
solutions.longDescription: This power bank battery management system provides complete charging, discharging, and monitoring functionality for portable power banks. The solution features bidirectional power flow - charging the internal battery from USB input and discharging to power external devices via USB output. The SC8905 bidirectional buck-boost converter enables efficient power conversion in both directions with up to 95% efficiency. The SC2731 fuel gauge provides accurate battery capacity monitoring and reporting. The SC5328 manages power path and charging control. Multiple protection features ensure safe operation including over-voltage, over-current, and over-temperature protection. The solution supports various battery configurations from single-cell to 4-cell series. USB PD and Quick Charge protocols can be supported for fast charging. As an authorized SouthChip distributor, LiTong provides complete power bank design support including reference designs and component selection.

coreProducts.reason: Buck-boost topology enables both charging and discharging with high efficiency
Accurate battery monitoring for capacity indication
Power path control and charging management

keyFeatures: Bidirectional power flow for charge and discharge

faeInsights.content: The power bank solution using SouthChip ICs is very effective. The SC8905 bidirectional converter is the key - it handles both charging the internal battery and discharging to external devices with excellent efficiency. Key design points: The inductor selection is critical for efficiency - use a low-DCR inductor rated for peak currents. Thermal design is important for sustained high-power operation - provide adequate copper area and vias. The fuel gauge calibration should be done with the actual battery cells for best accuracy. For multi-cell applications, add cell balancing circuitry. The USB PD protocol implementation requires a PD controller - SouthChip has reference designs showing integration. One common issue: ensure proper input/output capacitance for stability under load transients. Overall, this is a cost-effective, efficient solution for power bank applications.
keyTakeaways: SC8905 bidirectional converter is the core of the solution
Thermal design important for high-power operation
Fuel gauge calibration with actual cells for accuracy

steps: Determine power capacity and battery configuration
Design thermal management for sustained operation
Add USB PD controller for protocol support

faqs.question: What battery configurations are supported?
faqs.answer: The power bank solution supports various battery configurations: (1) Single-cell - 3.7V nominal, simplest design for small power banks (5000-10000mAh). (2) 2S (series) - 7.4V nominal, good for medium power banks with higher output voltage. (3) 3S - 11.1V nominal, for higher power applications. (4) 4S - 14.8V nominal, for high-capacity power stations. The SC8905 buck-boost topology handles the voltage conversion between battery and USB ports. For multi-cell configurations, add cell balancing and protection circuits. The fuel gauge supports multi-cell monitoring with appropriate scaling. LiTong can provide design guidance for different battery configurations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for battery configuration selection and multi-cell design guidance.

faqs.question: How do I implement USB PD protocol?
faqs.answer: USB PD implementation with SouthChip solution: (1) PD controller - Add USB PD controller IC (e.g., FUSB302B) to handle PD protocol communication on CC lines. (2) Voltage negotiation - PD controller negotiates voltage (5V, 9V, 12V, 15V, 20V) and current with connected devices. (3) Converter control - SC8905 adjusts output voltage based on PD negotiation result. (4) Power rules - Implement PD power rules for source/sink capabilities. (5) Safety - Include over-voltage and over-current protection compliant with PD specifications. (6) Firmware - Implement PD state machine in microcontroller. SouthChip provides reference designs showing PD integration. The combination of PD controller + SC8905 enables full USB PD functionality with programmable power supply (PPS) support on some implementations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for USB PD implementation and reference design access.

faqs.question: What power management solutions does SouthChip offer?
faqs.answer: SouthChip offers comprehensive power management solutions: (1) Smartphone Power Management - Complete power subsystem with fast charging, fuel gauging, and multi-rail DC-DC conversion. (2) Power Bank BMS - Bidirectional charging/discharging with high efficiency and accurate capacity indication. (3) Tablet Power Solutions - High-current charging and multi-rail power for larger tablets. (4) IoT Power Solutions - Ultra-low power management for battery-operated devices. (5) Industrial Power Solutions - Robust power management for harsh environments. Each solution is built from SouthChip's portfolio of battery chargers, fuel gauges, DC-DC converters, and power path management ICs. As an authorized SouthChip distributor, LiTong provides complete solution support including reference designs and technical assistance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for solution selection based on your application requirements.

faqs.question: How do I select the right SouthChip solution?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right SouthChip solution involves: (1) Define requirements - Charge current, system rails, battery capacity, size constraints. (2) Identify key components - Charger, fuel gauge, DC-DC converters, power management. (3) Evaluate solutions - Review existing solutions or design custom architecture. (4) Reference designs - Start with SouthChip reference designs for faster development. (5) Prototype - Build and test prototype with evaluation boards. (6) Optimization - Fine-tune for your specific requirements. LiTong FAEs can help analyze your requirements and recommend optimal SouthChip solutions. We provide schematic review, PCB layout guidance, and debugging support throughout your design cycle.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong FAEs for solution selection and design support.
